reclassification_note: v1 (AI output) used the v2_0 disposition catalogue which conflated 'belongs to another cluster's Session B' with 'truly cross-cluster Session D'. v2 applies v2_1 strict criterion: bilateral cluster relationships → ROUTE-TO-CLUSTER (handled by target cluster's T6); only ≥3-cluster programme-wide or methodological observations → CARRY-TO-SESSION-D. Researcher direction: Option III (CC re-classifies; no AI round-trip).

reconciliations.rationale: Bilateral pattern: abomination ↔ new-covenant transformation. Per v2_1 §13.2.1 decision tree, bilateral cluster relationships are handled by the target cluster's T6 prompts (T6.3 causal/constitutive), not Session D. Belongs to Abomination cluster's analysis.
reconciliations.target_v1_was: Session D — cross-registry analysis (abomination + new covenant transformation)
reconciliations.rationale_v1_was: Content concerns the abomination cluster's verse evidence and new covenant transformation as remedy for abomination. Not M01 content — no fear terms involved. Session D cross-registry defilement-redemption analysis is the appropriate scope.
reconciliations.reclassified_by: CC under researcher Option III decision (2026-05-16)

reconciliations.rationale: The finding asserts that the deepest fear/awe content integrates affective intensity and God-ward orientation inseparably. Phase 9 confirms this: T1.8.1 [A] identifies M01-A's primary dimension as affective-relational with multi-dimensional secondaries; T1.8.3 [A] confirms integration rather than competition between dimensions; T3.4.1 [A] confirms fear paired with joy and delight (Psa 2:11; Heb 12:28); T0.3.1 [A] establishes the God-ward orientation as constitutive of M01-A's image-bearing function. The finding's substance is captured.

reconciliations.rationale: The finding identifies fobeō's tripartite inner-being structure: theophanic fear to worship (M01-B-VCG-01/02), reverential life-shaping fear of God (M01-A), and practical self-interested fear (M01-B-VCG-03/04). Phase 9 covers all three: T1.4.2 [A, B, CLUSTER] documents object-dependent operation across all registers; T7.1.3 [A] confirms the semantic range spanning reverential awe to cringing alarm; T1.2.1 [A, B] distinguishes dispositional from episodic forms corresponding to the tripartite structure. The finding's analytical insight is captured and confirmed.

The finding identifies the divine 'Fear not' command pattern (ya.re H3372G group 298-001) as a distinct inner-being structuring element — divine presence declaration plus commanded displacement of fear. Phase 9 addresses this directly: T1.4.1 [B] names commanded release as a mode of M01-B's operation; T5.2.1 [B] describes the before-during-after sequence; T5.3.1 [B] identifies divine word as the operative mechanism; T4.1.1 [B] documents God commanding the release of fear. The entire M01-B-VCG-06 (anchor Isa 43:1) is the dedicated Phase 9 finding for this pattern.
Wonder cluster Session B work for source registry 175. No M01 fear terms. Belongs to Wonder cluster's analysis.
reconciliations.target_v1_was: Session D — wonder/astonishment cluster Session B analysis
reconciliations.rationale_v1_was: Content concerns wonder vocabulary (registry 175) as the inner state at the human-divine interface. Wonder terms (ta.mah, thambos) are in M01-BOUNDARY, noted in Phase 9 BOUNDARY characterisation for possible routing to a separate astonishment/wonder cluster. The finding's analytical scope belongs to that cluster's Session B. CARRY-TO-SESSION-D to allow the wonder/astonishment cluster to take up this question.
reconciliations.reclassified_by: CC under researcher Option III decision (2026-05-16)

reconciliations.label: DIM-61-SD001
reconciliations.target: Session D — tripartite dimension pattern methodology
reconciliations.rationale: Programme-wide methodological observation: does the orientation/affect/will-collapse tripartite pattern recur across negative-state clusters? Asks for cross-cluster testing. Belongs to Session D under v2_1 strict criterion.
reconciliations.target_v1_was: Session D — tripartite dimension pattern cross-cluster analysis (orientation/affect/will-collapse)
reconciliations.rationale_v1_was: SD_POINTER by designation. M01's tripartite pattern is confirmed in Phase 9: T1.8.1 [CLUSTER] (affective primary, volitional most consistent secondary), T3.6.1 [CLUSTER] (volitional faculty as primary operating theatre — will-collapse confirmed), T3.4.1 [CLUSTER] (affective dimension constitutive). Session D cross-cluster synthesis of whether other negative inner states share this structure is the appropriate scope.
reconciliations.reclassified_by: CC under researcher Option III decision (2026-05-16)
